domain: volcanology (magma chamber pressure equalization via dike intrusion)
specific_mechanism: When magma chamber pressure exceeds the surrounding host-rock fracture strength, magma intrudes as a dike — a narrow planar conduit — that propagates laterally or vertically until pressure equalizes; dike orientation is controlled by regional stress field; the propagation is a self-arresting process (as the dike grows, chamber pressure drops, propagation slows)
llm_application: When activation pressure on a single LLM circuit (gradient magnitude, attention entropy) exceeds a host-rock strength threshold, a 'dike' opens — a narrow planar pathway that propagates through the network until activation pressure equalizes; the propagation is self-arresting and the dike orientation is controlled by the local stress field of the parameter gradient. The mechanism is a structural release valve preventing single-circuit overload.
novelty_claim: Existing activation stabilization uses scalar regularization or layer-norm. No published 2024-2026 LLM/AI work formalizes a dike-style narrow self-arresting planar release pathway with orientation controlled by gradient stress field.
